summary: The clinical significance of bile duct sludge versus stones lies in their differing behaviors—sludge may precede stone formation without causing acute symptoms or needing direct intervention, while stones cause obstructive issues requiring urgent management like endoscopic extraction. This leads to distinct diagnostic pathways and treatment approaches based on whether an actual physical blockage exists.
